preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes context for trial users in decision stage — reaffirms what the product does and frames the page as a guide for their next step, appealing to the Explorer's desire for clarity before commitment.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Required by Explorer archetype. Showcases product capabilities and outcome promises, giving trial users a structured overview of what they've been exploring and what additional depth awaits — satisfying the Explorer's need to fully understand before deciding.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Required by Explorer archetype. Social proof and trust signals reassure trial users that others have validated the product, reducing perceived risk without applying pressure — letting Explorers draw their own conclusions from evidence of community trust.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required by decision funnel stage. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give trial users the quantitative confidence they need at the decision stage — Explorers value data-driven discovery over persuasion.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required by decision funnel stage. Presents pricing and plan comparison in a transparent, explorable format — framing the choice rather than forcing it, which respects the Explorer's autonomy while providing the structure needed at this funnel stage.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Must appear immediately before Recovery. The CTA is positioned after all evidence and decision framing so the Explorer feels informed rather than pressured — a natural culmination of their self-guided journey through the page.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths such as documentation, additional case studies, or a way to extend the trial — essential for Explorers who may not convert immediately but want to continue discovery without feeling abandoned.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Required by Explorer archetype. Showcases product capabilities and outcome promises, giving trial users a structured overview of what they've been exploring and what additional depth awaits — satisfying the Explorer's need to fully understand before deciding.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage trial users need focused conversion intent. The notes specify 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only,' which directly supports the goal to buy the product.

Explorer archetype requires deeper engagement. The notes state 'Explorer variant uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature,' providing the outcome-oriented descriptions needed for explorers evaluating product fit.

slots.headline: Everything the intelligence layer needs to ship
slots.subhead: MODE resolves behavior, visual emphasis, and content register automatically — across every archetype, funnel stage, and context mode your product encounters.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components don't just render — they resolve. The four-dimension model evaluates archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode on every pass, so the right behavior emerges without a single manual variant or conditional override.

features.title: IA planner agent
features.description: Go from a brief to a full page architecture in seconds. The planner, powered by Claude Opus, selects and sequences components with zone affinity and funnel logic built in — not guessed at after the fact.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Each variant reflects the behavioral token set for that archetype — copy density, evidence weighting, and CTA posture all resolve correctly without branching your codebase.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. The palette map assigns the right accent weight per component per funnel stage — no manual color decisions, no inconsistent hierarchy across pages.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: Eleven components fully specified with slot schemas, zone affinity, and usage intent. Every component knows where it belongs in the funnel and what it needs to communicate — the system enforces coherence by default.

features.title: Full ownership, no lock-in
features.description: MODE lives in your codebase. Bring your own Anthropic API key, run the token spend yourself, and fork the resolution engine freely. There is no runtime dependency, no vendor dashboard, and no subscription standing between your team and the intelligence layer.

page.reasoning: At decision stage with conversion goal, the with-cta variant is appropriate as notes indicate it 'recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
